content.full_text:
November 20, 1989


Judy Vonada
City Clerk
City of Hayward
22300 Foothill Boulevard
Hayward, CA  94541

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-89-605

Dear Ms.Vonada:

     You have requested advice on behalf of Hayward Mayor Alex
Giuliani regarding his obligations under the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").¹

QUESTION

     Mayor Giuliani has established a committee to run for the
Assembly in 1990.  May the committee solicit funds to pay for
legal fees in connection with proceedings to correct an error
which the Mayor made in changing his party affiliation in order to
run for the Assembly?²


1  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2  You also asked two other questions.  The first involved the
obligations of Mr. Giuliani's opponent.  As I indicated in our
telephone conversation, the Commission does not provide advice
regarding past conduct, regarding the duties of persons whom
the requestor does not represent.

     Second, you asked whether Mayor Giuliani's Assembly 1990
committee can use funds raised by the committee to pay legal fees
in connection with the litigation.  This question falls under
provisions of the Elections Code which are not presently under the
jurisdiction of the Commission.  Your question should be addressed
to the Attorney General's Office.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Our File No. A-89-605
Page 2


CONCLUSION

     Funds raised to pay the legal fees are contributions which
may be solicited by Mayor Giuliani's Assembly 1990 committee.
Such funds are subject to the contribution limits of Proposition
73.

FACTS

     Recently, Hayward Mayor Alex Giuliani decided to run for the
Assembly in 1990 as an independent.  As a result of this decision,
the Mayor changed his party affiliation to what he thought was an
"independent" however, by error he checked "American Independent".
The mistake on the Mayor's voter registration card was brought to
the attention of approximately 62,000 voters by the current
Assemblyman.  The Mayor is currently involved in legal proceedings
to correct the error and change his voter registration to
designate him as an "independent" rather than "American
Independent."  The action would enable him to run for Assembly as
an independent.

ANALYSIS

     The Act, as amended by Propositions 73, requires that prior
to soliciting or receiving contributions, an individual who
intends to be a candidate for an elective office must file with
the Commission a statement of intention to be a candidate for that
office (Form 501), and must establish one campaign contribution
account for that office.  (Sections 85200 and 85201.)  All
contributions for that office must be deposited in the account for
that office.  (Section 85201.)  Proposition 73 also imposes limits
on the amount of contributions which a candidate may accept from a
particular source in a single fiscal year.  (Sections 85301, 85303
and 85305.)

     Section 82015 provides that the term "contribution" includes
a payment for which full and adequate consideration is not
received, unless it is clear from the surrounding circumstances
that the payment is not made for political purposes.  Commission
regulations further define a contribution as a payment received by
or made at the behest of:

          A candidate, unless it is clear from surround-
          ing circumstances that the payment was received or
          made at his behest for personal purposes unrelated
          to his candidacy or status as an officeholder....

                    Regulation 18215(b)(1).

     In the present situation, the litigation to which you are
referring is directly related to Mayor Giuliani's candidacy for
the Assembly.  The funds received for that purpose constitute
contributions.  (In re Johnson (1989) 11 FPPC Ops. 1, In re
Buchanan (1979) 5 FPPC Ops. 14, copies enclosed.)  Accordingly,

Our File No. A-89-605
Page 3

they must be raised within the contribution limits of Proposition
73, and must be deposited in the account for Mayor Giuliani's race
for the Assembly in 1990.

If you have any further questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: John G. McLean
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JGM:aa

Enclosures

CITY OF HAYWARD.OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK

October 17, 1989

Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 I Street, Suite 800
Box 807
Sacramento, CA. 95804-0807

I would appreciate your assistance in answering several questions. However,
before I proceed with the questions, I think it is important to give you the
background.

Recently our Mayor Alex Giuliani decided to run for Assemblyman as an
independent in 1990. As a result of this decision the Mayor changed his party
affiliation to what he thought was an "independent" however, by error he
checked "American Independent". The mistake on the Mayor's voter registration
card was brought to the attention of approximately 62,000 registered voters by
the current Assemblyman. The Mayor is currently involved in legal proceedings
to correct the error and change his voter registration to designate him as an
"independent" not "American Independent". The action would enable him to run
for Assembly as an independent.

I would appreciate your assistance with the following questions:

1. Is there any regulation that covers the amount of funds the current
   Assemblyman used to mail out the information regarding the voter
   registration of his opponent, Alex Giuliani? (A copy of the material
   mailed is attached)

2. A committee to support Alex Giuliani's candidacy for Assemblyman has
   already been established. Can the funds from this committee be used to
   pay for the fees in connection with the proceedings to change his voter
   designation?

3. If these funds can be used to pay the legal fees, can the committee
   continue to solicit funds to pay for the legal fees?

I would appreciate a response as soon as possible. Thank you for your help.

Sincerely,

Judy Vonada
City Clerk

22300 FOOTHILL BOULEVARD, HAYWARD, CA 94541 • (415) 581-2345
HEARING IMPAIRED . TDD 537-7593
